mnemonics: Heine-Borel Helps: Closed & Bounded = Complete & Totally Bounded (in Rⁿ only!)
keyPoints: The Heine-Borel theorem states that a subset of ℝⁿ is compact if and only if it is closed and bounded.
This is a special property of ℝⁿ with the Euclidean metric and does not hold in general metric spaces.
It provides a simple, practical way to identify compact sets in finite-dimensional real space.
commonMistakes: Assuming the theorem holds in any metric space (it's specific to ℝⁿ).
Forgetting that both conditions (closed AND bounded) are necessary; one without the other is not sufficient.
Confusing the Heine-Borel characterization with the general definition of compactness (every open cover has a finite subcover).
